description: Path traversal and remote code execution in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.49 via mod_cgi and aliased paths, allowing access to files outside the document root.
Incomplete fix for CVE-2021-41773 in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.49 and 2.4.50, allowing path traversal and RCE via double-encoded path separators.
NULL pointer dereference in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.48 and earlier causing a denial of service when processing malformed requests.
Heap overflow in mod_session_crypto in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.0 to 2.4.46 when a specially crafted SessionHeader value is sent.
NULL pointer dereference in mod_session in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.0 to 2.4.46 via a crafted Cookie header, causing denial of service.
Buffer overflow in mod_lua multipart parser in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.51 and earlier, potentially allowing remote code execution.
Server-side request forgery (SSRF) in mod_proxy in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.48 and earlier via a crafted request URI.
Out-of-bounds write in ap_escape_quotes in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.48 and earlier via malicious input from modules.
Buffer overflow in mod_proxy_uwsgi in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.32 to 2.4.44, potentially leading to information disclosure or RCE.
HTTP/2 logging issue in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.20 to 2.4.43 causing memory corruption and denial of service.
HTTP/2 Cache-Digest header handling in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.20 to 2.4.43 can cause a crash via a specially crafted value.
Apache HTTP Server 2.4.17 to 2.4.38 privilege escalation from a worker process (CGI script or module) to the parent process running as root via the scoreboard.
Access control bypass in mod_ssl in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.37 and 2.4.38 with per-location client certificate verification and TLS 1.3.
Access control bypass in mod_auth_digest in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.38 and earlier due to a race condition, allowing a valid user to impersonate another.
Stack buffer overflow and NULL pointer dereference in mod_remoteip in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.32 to 2.4.39 via trusted intermediary proxies.
mod_auth_digest in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.0 to 2.4.29 reuses the client nonce, allowing replay attacks across servers in a cluster.
Apache HTTP Server 2.4.0 to 2.4.29 expression specified in FilesMatch could match a malicious filename ending in a newline, bypassing upload restrictions.
Optionsbleed: use-after-free in Apache HTTP Server when handling Limit directives in .htaccess, leaking memory via the OPTIONS method.
mod_mime in Apache HTTP Server 2.2.x to 2.2.32 and 2.4.x to 2.4.25 can read one byte past the end of a buffer when sending a malicious Content-Type response header.
Use of ap_get_basic_auth_pw by third-party modules outside the authentication phase in Apache HTTP Server 2.2.x to 2.2.32 and 2.4.x to 2.4.25 may lead to authentication bypass.
mod_ssl in Apache HTTP Server 2.2.x to 2.2.32 and 2.4.x to 2.4.25 may dereference a NULL pointer when third-party modules call ap_hook_process_connection during an HTTP request to an HTTPS port.
Apache HTTP Server before 2.2.32 and 2.4.x before 2.4.25 does not properly enforce HTTP request grammar, allowing request smuggling or response splitting with proxies.
httpoxy: Apache HTTP Server through 2.4.23 uses the Proxy header from a request to set the HTTP_PROXY environment variable for CGI scripts, enabling MITM redirection.
Race condition in mod_status in Apache HTTP Server before 2.4.10 allows attackers to cause a heap-based buffer overflow and possibly execute arbitrary code.
The log_cookie function in mod_log_config in Apache HTTP Server before 2.4.8 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service via a crafted truncated cookie.
mod_session_dbd in Apache HTTP Server before 2.4.5 does not properly maintain a session ID, with possible security implications for session persistence.
The log_cookie function in mod_log_config in Apache HTTP Server 2.2.17 to 2.2.21 allows denial of service via a cookie that lacks a value.
Apache Killer: the byterange filter in Apache HTTP Server 1.3.x, 2.0.x through 2.0.64, and 2.2.x through 2.2.19 allows remote denial of service via a Range header with many overlapping ranges.
mod_isapi in Apache HTTP Server 2.0.37 to 2.2.14 on Windows allows remote code execution via a crafted request that triggers unloading of an ISAPI .dll module.
Apache HTTP Server 2.4.52 and earlier fails to close inbound connections when discarding some request bodies, exposing the server to HTTP request smuggling.
If LimitXMLRequestBody is set very high in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.52 and earlier, an integer overflow in mod_dav can lead to a buffer overflow on 32-bit systems.
Out-of-bounds write in mod_sed in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.52 and earlier allows an attacker to overwrite heap memory with attacker-controlled data.
mod_proxy in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.53 and earlier may fail to send X-Forwarded-* headers to the origin server, allowing authentication bypass based on those headers.
HTTP request smuggling in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.0 to 2.4.55 with certain mod_proxy and RewriteRule/ProxyPassMatch configurations using non-specific substitutions.
HTTP response splitting in mod_proxy_uwsgi in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.30 to 2.4.55, allowing request smuggling to a backend via special characters in the origin response header.
Apache HTTP Server 2.4.59 and earlier may use backend application output to run local handlers via internal redirect, allowing code execution or SSRF with untrusted content.
Encoding problem in mod_proxy in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.59 and earlier allows request URLs with incorrect encoding to be sent to backend services, bypassing authentication.
HTTP/2 CONTINUATION flood: Apache HTTP Server with mod_http2 may exhaust memory when handling a stream of CONTINUATION frames without END_HEADERS, causing denial of service.
